items.title: Eddie Jones suspended by Japan rugby for verbal abuse on Australian tour

items.summary: Donald Trump is expected to ask for China's help to resolve the costly and unpopular conflict with Iran, but analysts say he is unlikely to get the support he wants. Follow live.

items.summary: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.

items.title: Izak Rankine says he has bounced back from suspension for homophobic slur
items.summary: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

items.title: Porepunkah property owner speaks in video after fatal police shooting
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded officer after Dezi Freeman shot at police
items.summary: A Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded police officer after the fatal shooting when Dezi Freeman killed two Victoria police officers.

items.title: Emotional Senate tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby amid debate on focus of NT inquiry
items.summary: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.

items.title: Man charged with son's torture and murder previously offered to plead guilty to manslaughter, court hears
items.summary: Reinhardt Bosch has been charged with the murder and torture of his son Rhuan in 2022, and offered to plead guilty to the lesser charge of manslaughter, a court has heard.

items.title: Murderer sentenced to life in prison over death of 22yo Dale Aaron McKenna
items.summary: The court in Toowoomba heard Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe was motivated by revenge after discovering his on-again off-again girlfriend had started a relationship with the 22-year-old victim.

items.title: Sydney cage fighter found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing best friend
items.summary: Bradley Fletcher has been found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing his best friend during a backyard sparring session and leaving his body by a footpath.

items.title: Yindjibarndi people win $150m compensation after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue mines built without permission
items.summary: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

items.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
items.summary: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

items.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report with budget day release
items.summary: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

items.title: Tax reform to help 75,000 buyers into first homes, government says ahead of federal budget's release
items.summary: In a political push to get ahead of criticism it has broken election promises, Labor is making a case for how wealth is taxed in Australia by promising the budget will open the housing market to younger buyers.

items.title: Holmes à Court family-owned Heytesbury buys 11 live export ships in major deal with Vroon
items.summary: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

items.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access
items.summary: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

items.title: Canberra teen charged with planning terrorist attack and transmitting violent extremist material
items.summary: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Children's Court today charged with one count of planning or preparing for a terrorist act and one count of transmitting violent extremist material.

items.title: Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie uses parliamentary privilege to attack Labor MPs
items.summary: It comes after more than a week of scrutiny on ministerial lovers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm.

items.title: Canvas developer Instructure says 'agreement' reached with hackers
items.summary: The company at the centre of a cyber attack involving online learning platform Canvas, which is used right across Australia, says it has reached an "agreement" with the hackers responsible.

items.title: Jack Alex Cullen jailed for trafficking cocaine while in Australian Defence Force
items.summary: The Supreme Court of Queensland hears Cullen, a former infantry soldier, was sourcing ounces of cocaine and selling it to fellow soldiers while in the Australian Army.

items.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi

items.title: Racist attacks leave New Zealand's Indian community feeling 'hounded'

items.title: What giving up alcohol taught me about myself
items.summary: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

items.title: Prosecutors drop murder, manslaughter charges against Isla Bell's accused killer Marat Ganiev
items.summary: Melbourne man Marat Ganiev, who was accused of killing 19-year-old Isla Bell and putting her remains in a fridge, was due to face a Supreme Court trial this month.

items.title: Australian government finalising plans to bring hantavirus cruise ship passengers home
items.summary: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.

items.title: Jessica Pegula putting family experience in NFL and NHL to use in tennis prize money protest

items.title: Man arrested after allegedly setting another on fire in Warrawong unit
items.summary: Emergency service crews were called to an apartment complex in the Wollongong suburb of Warrawong overnight after a 25-year-old man was set on fire.

items.title: Man charged over allegedly robbing cash transit vehicle with weapon in Hervey Bay

items.title: Alleged driver charged over deaths of two girls in Viveash crash
items.summary: Police charge a 24-year-old man with dangerous driving causing death, a fortnight after two girls died from the injuries they suffered when their car smashed into a tree in Viveash.

items.title: Darwin chemical recycler prepares to make diesel from gas condensate
items.summary: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

items.title: Coach Michael Voss resigns from AFL strugglers Carlton, Mick Malthouse says premiership winning player will be relieved
items.summary: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

items.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
items.summary: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

items.title: ASX slips as budget comes into focus and DroneShield faces ASIC probe — as it happened
items.summary: Australian shares slid while DroneShield was being investigated by ASIC over disclosures and share trading. Look back on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our live blog.

items.title: Volunteer gives central Queensland hospice residents free haircuts
items.summary: Discussing how people want to be treated at the end of their life can be hard, but experts say planning can bring dignity and comfort.

items.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
items.summary: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

items.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
items.summary: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.

items.title: Alexei Popyrin brushed aside by world number one Jannik Sinner at Italian Open
items.summary: Alexei Popyrin has been outclassed in Rome, beaten 6-2 6-0 by world number one Jannik Sinner in 65 one-sided minutes in the Italian Open third round.

items.title: Apple's Tim Cook and Tesla's Elon Musk among top US CEOs to accompany Trump to China
items.summary: According to a list shared by a White House official, speaking on condition of anonymity, Elon Musk and Tim Cook will be accompanied by 15 other chief executive officers when Donald Trump visits China.

items.summary: The treasurer unveils a "reforming" federal budget with sweeping changes, including a tax cut for workers. Take a look back at the day's coverage here.

items.title: Small robots used to help students consider careers in renewable energy
items.summary: Students are using tiny, programmable robots to code in a bid to encourage them to consider careers in renewable energy due to predicted skill shortages.

items.title: Students criticise Adelaide University's communication over Canvas cybersecurity breach
items.summary: Adelaide University says access to the cloud-based learning platform Canvas is back online after a data breach last week, but some students have criticised the institution's communication about the incident.

items.title: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to develop over Australia, delivering a broad band of rain
items.summary: In a welcome twist of weather variability, the first north-west cloudband of 2026 will finally develop over Australia this week, delivering a broad band of rain.

items.title: Queensland man pioneers app to fly drones using head movements
items.summary: Ever since he was a young child, Christopher Hills has wanted to fly an aircraft. Now he has made that dream a reality.

items.title: Study finds link between air pollution exposure and risk of ending up in hospital
items.summary: A new study has found a clear link between air pollution exposure and the risk of ending up in hospital. It's a battle Julia Ovens knows only too well.

items.title: Homes Tasmania might come and go before Felicity gets a rental
items.summary: Felicity Hirst says she is not too picky about what kind of house she lives in; she just wants one. She has been on Tasmania's social housing list for three and a half years — as long as the state's housing authority has existed.

items.title: Trump and Xi's Beijing summit must confront the AI cold war
items.summary: When Trump last visited China in 2017, artificial intelligence was not yet the centre of global power. Now it is.

items.title: Cricket Australia hopes Big Bash League privatisation could make it one of sport's 'grand slams'
items.summary: Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg says private investment in the Big Bash League is "inevitable" and waiting for too long could mean "we've missed the boat" as more competitions pop up around the world.

items.title: Vanuatu's cabinet approves new version of Nakamal Agreement with Australia
items.summary: The ABC can reveal that after months of difficult negotiations, the Nakamal Agreement has been approved by Vanuatu's cabinet but omits key limitations on China's influence.

items.title: More or Less quiz: Guess where your money goes, and what it gets spent on

items.title: What will be in tonight's federal budget? Here are the major measures we know about so far

items.title: The caring burden for single, childless women
items.summary: Single women over 45 without children are much more likely to be spending time caring for family members who are aging, have a disability or chronic illness. And it's not just costing them financially.

items.title: How Mortal Kombat II brought Hollywood to the Gold Coast
items.summary: The $110 million Mortal Kombat sequel is the next in a laundry list of productions putting Queensland on the radar for international films.

items.title: Keir Starmer vows to prove 'doubters' wrong as UK Labour MPs demand his exit
items.summary: The British prime minister is fighting to fend off a growing threat to his leadership after a disastrous set of local election results last week.

items.title: US, Iran reach stalemate in war negotiations dashing hopes of Strait of Hormuz reopening
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he's aiming for "complete victory" in Iran as the US and Iran reach an impasse in negotiations.

items.title: Deputy Liberal leader Jane Hume promises new Coalition after Farrer by-election loss
items.summary: Deputy Liberal leader Jane Hume is promising a new day for a Coalition that has lost its way, but whether the party can recover from the wreckage of Farrer remains to be seen.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Hadley's shock return 10 weeks post-baby
items.summary: Paige Hadley made a shock return to the Super Netball league less than three months after giving birth to her baby boy. Despite some commentary online questioning her priorities, the midcourter is more determined than ever to prove women can pursue multiple dreams.
